data.label: I explore the storytelling workshop and I meet some interesting people there.
Through that I meet a woman that maybe I hang out with and think about dating.
My social safety net is tested; it's okay — I don't get totally wrecked.
I kind of make more connections in Tucson.
I find a good third place that I feel comfortable in that doesn't have some of the drawbacks of circling.
When my birthday in May 2026 comes around I have a party with more people than the last one and it's enjoyable and my current friends and my new friends get along.
That gives me a springboard to play with some of my ideas and facilitation.
I go to social things and it's overwhelming and boring and it diffuses my attention.
I long for being around my friends in the Bay which I simultaneously can't really handle because of the psychedelic intensity and expensiveness.
My work continues to flounder and by my birthday I'm just like this is the same story that I've been in; I didn't learn anything from Michael's death.
I'm still a workaholic, I still have a bunch of superficial friendships, I still aren't where I belong and my dreams are slipping away from me.
I don't really do any more social activities than normal.
Women kind of are disappointed in me cuz I'm like both interested but not taking any action.
The image I have of myself as a bold go-getter is just not who I am anymore.
I read a bunch of stuff online and watch a bunch of YouTubes and read a bunch of books; I develop my erudition; I have clever things to say.
I experiment with facilitation sometimes in the circling community; I still feel annoyed people don't get me and that there feels like there's more for me in life.
Things are okay. I meditate. I have moments of peace. I go on adventures.
Over time the stuff that I'm already doing with my health compounds and I work my way through the throat pain — just waiting.
I start meeting with my coworkers more.

I call it staying in contact by staying in contact with Thomas the salesperson, Cindre the security professional, Danny the back end ops maven, Simon the design guru, Alex the data science wiz, and Leo the overworked computer science genius.
My connection with everyone helps stabilize and amplify our coherence as a team.
I let go of my aspirations to do something super cool in terms of my engineering and I focus on the simple consensus that I form with Danny and Simon.
Together we power a road map that actually delivers on things that make sense to the sales team.
I say f*** Leo's micromanagement; I am going to engage and fill in the psychosocial emotional gaps of this team.
With Simon who is on the same page as me, we focus the product into something that makes sense.
The fear and frustration of Alex and Leo and Thomas are allayed because we deliver on the core experience that makes this product useful and interesting.

The product begins to get external success and my theoretical underpinnings are amplified because underneath what we're building is an information processing system and social features are f****** amazing.
The main experiment is a connection board: I give myself a star every week that I create a meeting outside of the normal process.
Expectations on myself wear on my energy; I get into my ADHD loops.
The product continues to suck; there's dissatisfaction and disappointment.

I can't move forward on the product because Alex Maurice is asking me to merge unfinished code and meanwhile Leo is off in his own fantasy fear world and asking for all kinds of funny research things when in reality we just aren't there.
My extra effort to talk to people leads to some initial gains, but I can't actually sustain the maker-manager requirements and my attention goes elsewhere.
Simon doesn't get the theoretical things and it's a f****** bummer.
I don't do anything about this; I just kind of go with the flow.
The team has a certain momentum; we've actually been making a lot of headway on these issues.
Whether I meet with more people or not, there's disappointment and fear that I'm not going to get my ideas to live through them — that I won't be seen.
I don't understand why Leo doesn't want me in product conversations as I f****** have been there since the beginning and I have a bunch of insights into the product.
I was pushing on Simon about f****** cyber use cases; meanwhile he's f****** complaining and I have a whole goddamn folder of these goddamn f****** things.
Just f****** share it means that I set the goal of not having my website finalized; even just a Google Doc with whatever project is currently on my mind.
I put it on Substack; I don't even use my website at first because the main thing is just getting the muscle down.
All I do with my website is clear out the weird stuff on the front end and do a quick just f****** share it; then I'm unblocked from starting to communicate with the wider world.
I just want to say hey, I'm a prototyper and an independent researcher and I like these topics; here's some s*** that I've made.
After I've built a little muscle of just f****** sharing it, then I take the time to get my website a bit more organized when I actually have s*** that I'm ready to just f****** share.
That frees up my energy; I can start collaborating with external people because I'm clear on who I am.
I have a work cycle aligned with my ADHD time cycles of 2 weeks; in one or two weeks I distill some s***; I'm not going to try to make anything very big at this moment.

By next May I have built some muscle and some friends that I'm sharing my writings with; we're riffing and building some online community; researchers see me as a creative guy with some wacky beliefs but also a solid thinker.
I try to just share it but when I do there's no one that's interested in it.
It gets stuck at the sharer stage and I've made a few different things but they're incoherent.
I end up in the exact same pattern about trying to build something too big rather than having a good short cycle momentum.
I don't do anything here and just let it kind of be how it is; sometimes I get really excited about doing something in person with people.
The rest of my oeuvre is like how my dad does — it just sort of piles up all these different experiments I can talk to people about.
My work is actually sufficient to be the place where things I work on have long-term thing and the people I work with help me compensate for this pattern.
It's disappointing and I'm jealous of all these people that have their creative life out there.
I'm too much of a perfectionist; maybe in another year or two other parts of my life move forward and when I have some more financial time I can actually develop some of these ideas.
